title: Deployed Infrastructure v2 - Clear Top Zone
scene_prompt: Cinematic documentary photograph of a large command center control room. The upper 45 percent of the frame is an expansive high ceiling area — clean white-gray drywall or painted concrete, minimal recessed lighting fixtures, and a wide uninterrupted plane of neutral light tones that reads almost as a solid pale field. This upper zone is intentionally sparse and even-toned to support text overlay. Below this clean ceiling expanse, a dramatic wall of large monitoring displays shows network topology maps, geographic dashboards, and real-time telemetry data in blue and amber tones. In front of the display wall, rows of operator workstations are arranged in tiered semicircular formation with the backs of seated figures visible. The boundary between the empty ceiling zone and the display wall creates a natural horizon line at roughly 45 percent from the top. Shot on 35mm lens, f/2.8 aperture. Kodak Portra 800 film stock. Warm analog color palette — forest green (#0f4c3a) shadows on equipment, cool blue from displays, warm amber from overhead ambient. Fine organic film grain. Natural vignetting. Editorial documentary quality. Even exposure — the ceiling zone should be bright and uniform, not dark. The image feels like overlooking a nerve center of critical infrastructure operations. No typography, no logos, no words, no lettering of any kind, no identifiable human faces.
palette: forest green (#0f4c3a) shadows, cool blue displays, warm amber ambient
composition: Upper 45% clean bright ceiling for text overlay. Lower 55% display wall and workstations. Clear horizon line between zones.
